us-politics.headline: US Jets Downed, Iran Threats Escalate as Deadline Nears
us-politics.body:
US military aircraft have been shot down by enemy fire for the first time in over 20 years during the ongoing conflict with Iran, marking a sharp escalation. Trump has publicly declared the war "near completion" while simultaneously raising new threats, and Sen. Lindsey Graham is urging Tehran to "choose wisely" as a diplomatic deadline approaches.

Analysts and opinion writers are divided on what "finishing the job" actually means in practice, with some arguing the US can prevail through attrition without ground troops. Secretary Rubio revoked the green cards of two relatives of slain Iranian general Qasem Soleimani living in the US, who were subsequently arrested by ICE. A Fox News commentary frames Trump as shouldering the burdens of a wartime presidency, while critics question the strategy's endgame.

sources.title: Sunday shows preview: Trump ups threats on Iran after saying war ‘near completion’
US military jets hit in Iran war are the first shot down by enemy fire in over 20 years
The US can beat Iran at its own game, and without boots on the ground
ICE arrests relatives of slain Iranian general Soleimani living in US after Rubio revokes their green cards
Rubio revokes green cards of 2 Soleimani relatives amid Iran conflict
Graham backs Trump’s Iran ultimatum as deadline approaches: ‘Choose wisely’
In Iran, what does ‘finish the job’ even mean?
TEVI TROY: Trump faces the burdens of a wartime presidency

us-politics.headline: Judge Blocks Trump College Admissions Race Compliance Order
us-politics.body:
A federal judge has halted the Trump administration's effort to force colleges to certify they are not considering race in admissions decisions, a move that followed the Supreme Court's 2023 ban on affirmative action. The administration had threatened to pull federal funding from institutions that could not prove compliance.

Supporters of the order argue it is a necessary enforcement mechanism to ensure universities follow the law. Critics, including civil liberties groups, say the certification requirement was vague and coercive. The ruling is a setback for the administration's broader push to reshape higher education policy, though an appeal is widely expected.

sources.title: Judge blocks Trump bid to require colleges to prove race not considered in admissions
Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Proposes Defense Surge, Deep Domestic Cuts
us-politics.body:
The White House has unveiled a budget proposal seeking a $1.5 trillion increase in defense spending while targeting significant reductions across domestic programs. The plan reflects the administration's stated priority of military strength, particularly as the US is engaged in active conflict with Iran.

Republicans broadly support the defense buildup, though former White House press secretary Kayleigh McEnany has warned that the midterms "will be hard" for the party, signaling internal concern about the political cost of domestic cuts. Democrats are expected to attack the proposal as harmful to social programs. The budget sets up a major congressional fight heading into the 2026 election cycle.

sources.title: Trump unveils $1.5T defense surge, deep domestic cuts — what’s on the budget chopping block
McEnany sounds alarm: Midterms ‘will be hard’ for Republicans

us-politics.headline: White House Ballroom Fight, Wisconsin Race Signal Political Tensions
us-politics.body:
Trump has filed an emergency appeal to continue construction of a ballroom inside the White House complex after a judge blocked the project, with critics arguing the renovation raises legal and ethical questions about use of public property. The dispute adds to a string of legal battles surrounding the administration.

Separately, Wisconsin Republicans are struggling ahead of a state Supreme Court race, spotlighting the party's difficulties in competitive state-level contests. A new poll shows a narrow majority of Virginians support redistricting to favor Democrats. China has also surpassed the US in global approval ratings according to a new Gallup survey, a finding that cuts across partisan lines on questions of American standing abroad.

sources.title: Wisconsin judicial race spotlights state GOP’s struggles
What we know about the White House ballroom bunker
Trump files emergency appeal to keep building White House ballroom
China now tops US in global approval ratings: Gallup
Narrow majority in Virginia backs redistricting to favor Democrats: Poll

au-politics.headline: Murder charge, police shooting mark violent Easter weekend
au-politics.body:
A man arrested at Melbourne Airport attempting to board an international flight has been charged with murdering a woman, with police intervening before he could leave the country. In Tasmania, a separate incident left a man in serious condition after being shot by police in Goodwood, southern Hobart. Details on the circumstances of the Tasmania shooting remain limited, with authorities yet to release a full account.

The cases add to a grim Easter period. Three people died and two remain critical following a spate of crashes on Queensland roads over the holiday weekend. Authorities have not yet indicated whether charges will follow the Queensland incidents.

sources.title: Man arrested attempting to board international flight at Melbourne airport charged with murdering woman
Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police
Three dead, two critical in spate of crashes on Queensland roads

au-politics.headline: Victoria Police face lawsuit over officer killed by Freeman
au-politics.body:
The sisters of Senior Constable Neal Thompson, killed by Dezi Freeman, are suing Victoria Police. The legal action signals the family holds the force partly responsible for the circumstances surrounding Thompson's death, though the specific allegations have not yet been detailed publicly.

The case is likely to intensify scrutiny of police procedures and duty-of-care obligations. Victoria Police has faced sustained pressure over officer safety protocols in recent years. The outcome could have broad implications for how the force manages risk and supports frontline officers.

sources.title: Sisters of officer killed by Dezi Freeman to sue Victoria Police

au-politics.headline: Fuel costs bite as WA and NSW tighten price oversight
au-politics.body:
Western Australia is expanding its FuelWatch scheme from May, requiring all regional service stations to report prices - a move the government says will improve transparency and competition in areas where motorists have few alternatives. In New South Wales, authorities are urging drivers to report suspected price gouging through the FuelCheck platform, backed by fresh funding.

The pressure is real. Some Australians say rising fuel costs are forcing changes to how they commute, and that food is becoming a luxury. Both state governments are leaning on disclosure and public reporting rather than direct price controls, a distinction critics may question if costs keep climbing.

sources.title: All WA regional stations must report fuel prices from May as FuelWatch scheme expands
Motorists urged to dob in service stations over fuel price hikes
Fuel prices force commute changes, as food becomes 'privilege' for some

au-politics.headline: Victoria First Nations treaty elections begin; Gaza flotilla returns
au-politics.body:
Victoria's First Nations treaty body is holding its inaugural elections, a milestone in the state's treaty process. Advocates describe it as communities asserting authority over negotiations that directly affect them. The elections mark a concrete step forward after years of groundwork, though the broader treaty process still has a long road ahead.

Separately, Australian activists are preparing to rejoin a flotilla heading to Gaza despite the ongoing conflict. One participant asked publicly whether they would face punishment for returning. The effort reflects continued pressure from parts of Australian civil society over the humanitarian situation in Gaza, a question that remains politically contested across party lines.

sources.title: ‘Will I be punished for daring to return?’ Australian flotilla activists to head back to Gaza as wider war rages
‘We’re the experts in our own lives’: Victoria’s First Nations treaty body is holding its first elections

world.headline: US-Israel Strike Iran as Tehran Rejects Trump Ultimatum
world.body:
The United States and Israel have escalated military strikes on Iranian infrastructure, hitting universities, bridges, and a psychiatric hospital in Tehran. Trump gave Iran a 48-hour deadline to negotiate, threatening to unleash "hell" if it refused. Tehran rejected the ultimatum outright.

A US fighter jet was shot down over Iran, and the search for the missing airman continues - presenting what analysts call a serious test for Washington. The US has arrested relatives of slain Iranian general Qasem Soleimani, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio personally stripping Soleimani's niece of her US residency. Planet Labs, a US satellite firm, announced a blackout on imagery related to the conflict.

sources.title: US satellite firm Planet Labs announces blackout on war on Iran images
Iran war live: Tehran rejects Trump’s ultimatum; fire at Kuwait oil complex
US and Iran trade threats to unleash 'hell' as search for missing US airman continues
US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
Search for missing airman presents serious test for US
What we know so far about the US fighter jet shot down over Iran
Tehran psychiatric hospital not usable after US-Israeli strike
Universities hit as US, Israel ramp up attacks on Iran’s infrastructure
Marco Rubio says he has stripped Qassem Soleimani’s niece of US residency
New video reveals moment US-Israeli munitions impact Iranian bridge
Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal as search for US pilot continues

world.headline: Iran War Ripples Through Region's Energy and Security
world.body:
Iran declared Iraqi ships free to transit the Strait of Hormuz as shipping movements through the critical waterway increased, signalling Tehran's effort to manage regional alliances while under attack. A fire broke out at a Kuwait oil complex, adding to energy market anxiety. Italy's Prime Minister Meloni flew to Qatar to discuss energy security with the emir.

Pakistan faces mounting pressure over how to navigate the fallout from a war on its doorstep. Indonesia received the bodies of UN peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon, a reminder of the broader regional toll. Iran has tightened coastal defences, with new footage showing the architecture of its maritime control.

sources.title: Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up
Iran war live: Tehran rejects Trump’s ultimatum; fire at Kuwait oil complex
How will Pakistan deal with the fallout from Iran war?
Indonesia receives bodies of peacekeepers killed in southern Lebanon
Iran’s coastline: architecture of control
Italy’s Meloni meets Qatar emir to discuss energy issues amid Iran war

world.headline: Israel Cracks Down on Protesters and Hebron Mosque
world.body: Israeli police arrested antiwar demonstrators in Tel Aviv as domestic opposition to the country's military campaigns grows. In the occupied West Bank, Israeli authorities tightened restrictions on the Ibrahimi Mosque in Hebron, further limiting Palestinian access to one of the region's most contested religious sites. Israeli drone strikes continued in central Gaza, with footage showing a vehicle engulfed in flames.

sources.title: Israeli measures tighten grip on Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque
Israeli police arrest antiwar protesters in Tel Aviv
Vehicle engulfed in flames after Israeli drone strike in central Gaza

world.headline: Germany Eyes Military Travel Rules; Russia Kills Five in Ukraine
world.body:
Germany is considering requiring men under 45 to obtain military approval before taking extended stays abroad, a move reflecting Europe's sharpening focus on defence readiness amid ongoing continental instability. The proposal has drawn significant public debate.

In Ukraine, a Russian strike on a market killed five people. Separately, the world's oldest serving leader is set to appoint a deputy for the first time in his 43-year rule, while a car ploughed into a Lao New Year parade in Louisiana, injuring around 15 people.

sources.title: German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad
Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
Car slams into Louisiana Lao New Year parade, injuring about 15 people

ai.headline: Claude Code Leak Spawns Malware, Pricing Dispute Follows
ai.body:
Anthropic is dealing with two Claude Code crises at once. Hackers are distributing copies of the leaked Claude Code source alongside malware payloads, turning the breach into an active security threat for anyone who downloads unofficial versions.

Meanwhile, Anthropic confirmed that Claude Code subscribers will face additional charges to use OpenClaw, a move that has frustrated developers who expected broader access under existing plans. The pricing change lands at a bad moment, with trust in the platform already strained by the leak. Together, the incidents put Anthropic on the defensive over both security and commercial practices.

sources.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
Hackers Are Posting the Claude Code Leak With Bonus Malware

ai.headline: AI Fakes and Copyright Trolls Are Targeting Independent Musicians
ai.body:
Folk musician Murphy Campbell found herself caught between two modern threats: AI-generated fakes mimicking her voice and a copyright troll exploiting the chaos to file claims against her own work. The Verge's account illustrates how independent artists face a compounding problem - synthetic media muddies ownership, and bad actors use that confusion as cover.

Separately, The Verge reports growing pressure on creators to prove their work is human-made, with some platforms and clients now demanding certification. The burden of proof has quietly shifted onto artists. That's a structural change, not a trend.

sources.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
Really, you made this without AI? Prove it

ai.headline: Linux 7.0 Cuts PostgreSQL Performance in Half on AWS
ai.body:
An AWS engineer has reported that upgrading to Linux 7.0 roughly halves PostgreSQL performance, and a quick fix looks unlikely. The regression appears tied to low-level kernel changes affecting how the database handles I/O, putting production workloads at risk for teams that have already upgraded.

The finding is significant because Linux kernel upgrades are often treated as routine. This one isn't. AWS and the broader PostgreSQL community now face pressure to either patch around the kernel behavior or wait on an upstream fix, with no clear timeline on either path.

sources.title: AWS Engineer Reports PostgreSQL Perf Halved by Linux 7.0, Fix May Not Be Easy

ai.headline: Anthropic Studies Emotion in Claude, Microsoft Drowns in Copilots
ai.body:
Anthropic published research examining how emotion concepts function inside Claude, finding that the model develops internal representations that behave similarly to emotional states during processing. The paper stops short of claiming sentience but adds weight to ongoing debates about what large language models are actually doing under the hood.

On a lighter note, an analysis of Microsoft's product lineup found the company has applied the Copilot name to a bewildering number of distinct products. The branding sprawl reflects a broader industry rush to attach AI labels to everything - a strategy that risks meaning nothing at all.

sources.title: How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'?
Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model

btc.headline: Bitcoin Gains Political and Institutional Credibility as Safe Haven
btc.body:
A former UK Chancellor has publicly backed Bitcoin as an alternative to what he calls failing financial systems, adding political weight to a growing institutional narrative. Research from Mercado Bitcoin reinforces the case: Bitcoin has historically outperformed both gold and equities in the months following major global shocks.

The timing matters. With macro uncertainty elevated, the argument that Bitcoin functions as a crisis asset is moving from crypto-native circles into mainstream finance and politics. That shift in who is making the argument may prove as significant as the argument itself.

sources.title: Bitcoin tends to outperform gold and stocks after global shocks, Mercado Bitcoin finds
Ex-UK Chancellor backs bitcoin as alternative to failing systems

btc.headline: Bitcoin's Node Infrastructure Needs a Conservative Overhaul, Says Song
btc.body:
Developer and author Jimmy Song is calling for a dedicated 'conservative' Bitcoin node client, one that prioritizes stability and resistance to change over feature development. His argument centers on the risk that the existing client ecosystem is too susceptible to well-intentioned but potentially destabilizing upgrades.

The proposal reflects a broader tension inside Bitcoin development between ossification advocates and those pushing for continued protocol evolution. Song's position is that Bitcoin's value depends on predictability, and that a client explicitly designed to resist change would serve as a meaningful check on that risk.

sources.title: Jimmy Song explains why Bitcoin needs a 'conservative' node client

btc.headline: Digital Asset Treasuries Face Pressure to Generate Real Returns
btc.body:
Corporate and institutional holders of digital assets are under growing pressure to do more than simply hold Bitcoin on a balance sheet. A new opinion piece argues that passive treasury strategies are losing their edge as the novelty premium fades and shareholders demand yield or strategic utility.

The critique lands at a pivotal moment. Several firms built their identities around Bitcoin accumulation, but with the trade now crowded and scrutiny rising, the next phase likely requires active treasury management rather than a buy-and-hold posture.

sources.title: Digital asset treasuries must now earn their keep

btc.headline: Iran's Telegram Ban Backfires as Durov Claims Surge in Usage
btc.body:
Telegram founder Pavel Durov says Iran's attempt to block the platform has produced the opposite of its intended effect, driving a surge in user activity rather than suppressing it. Durov framed the ban as evidence that government-imposed restrictions on communication tools tend to accelerate adoption.

The claim carries relevance for crypto. Telegram remains a primary organizing and communication layer for much of the crypto industry, and its resilience against state-level censorship directly affects how decentralized communities operate in restricted markets.

sources.title: Telegram founder Pavel Durov says Iranian government's ban backfired

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Mushroom Galette
food.description: A free-form savory tart with caramelized leeks and shiitake mushrooms bound by a white miso butter glaze on a flaky rough-puff pastry base.
food.tip: Chill your butter in the freezer for 15 minutes before making the pastry and handle it as little as possible — visible butter streaks in the dough are what create the flaky layers.

exercise.name: The Sunday Tempo Ladder
exercise.equipment: Running shoes, a flat route or treadmill
exercise.description: A structured run that builds through four 8-minute blocks at progressively faster paces — easy jog, comfortable, comfortably hard, and tempo — with 2-minute walk recoveries between each, training your body to shift gears rather than just log miles.

learn.topic: Taphonomy — the science of how things become fossils
learn.description: Taphonomy studies the journey an organism takes from death to discovery, covering decay, burial, mineralisation, and the extraordinary filters of chance that determine what the fossil record actually shows us.
learn.why: It reframes everything you think you know about prehistoric life — most soft-bodied creatures left no trace at all, meaning our picture of ancient ecosystems is radically incomplete in ways that are themselves scientifically fascinating to map.
